faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Gowanus home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, silky webbing in closet corners, small cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying around closets or storage areas. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or stored blankets. Our Gowanus technicians can perform a thorough inspection to confirm the presence of clothes moths.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control service include in Gowanus?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed inspection of closets, storage areas, and potential breeding sites, identification of moth species, targeted treatment using safe and effective methods, removal of larvae and eggs, preventive recommendations, and follow-up visits as needed. We also provide guidance on proper garment storage and maintenance to prevent future infestations in your Gowanus property.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control pricing vary from $550 to $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your Gowanus property, the number of affected areas, and the treatment methods required. A minor infestation in a single closet will cost significantly less than treating an entire brownstone with multiple storage areas. We provide free estimates and customize our approach to your specific situation and budget.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Gowanus home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, dry cleaning or washing garments before storage, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our technicians provide specific recommendations based on your Gowanus property's conditions and will schedule preventive maintenance visits to ensure long-term protection of your valuable textiles.
